channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Milton destroys the roof of the Rays' stadium, littering the field below with debris
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — The roof panels over the home of baseball's Tampa Bay Rays were ripped to shreds by the force of Hurricane Milton, with debris littering the field and much of the seating areas Thursday after the deadly storm barreled across the state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former England manager Gareth Southgate says he won't coach in the next year
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Former England coach Gareth Southgate said Thursday he will be taking a year away from a club or national team job, after speculation he could be a target for Manchester United. “I won’t coach in the next year, for sure.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 22-time Grand Slam champion Rafael Nadal will retire from tennis after next month's Davis Cup finals
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Rafael Nadal is retiring from professional tennis at age 38, he announced in a video message Thursday, after winning 22 Grand Slam titles — 14 at the French Open — during an unprecedented era he shared with rivals Roger Federer and Novak Djokovic.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI and HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Phillies eliminated from playoffs after 4-1 loss to Mets in Game 4 of NLDS
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Francisco Lindor hit a grand slam in the sixth inning, his latest clutch swing in a storybook season full of them, and the New York Mets reached the National League Championship Series with a 4-1 victory over the Philadelphia Phillies on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How much are Padres tickets?
item.description: Are you hoping to cheer on the Padres from the stands of Petco Park for Game 4 of the National League Division Series?
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, Fox 5

item.title: Mets postseason tickets outsell Yankees on StubHub
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Mets and Yankees are both looking to make a run this postseason, but the Amazins are selling more tickets than the Bronx Bombers, according to StubHub. The Mets-Phillies National League Division Series was the best-selling series of the 2024 postseason on StubHub.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Phillies one defeat from elimination after 7-2 loss to Mets in Game 3 of NLDS
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Six months of success for the Philadelphia Phillies could be wiped out before the leaves start to turn. With one more loss to the New York Mets, a 95-win regular season would become an afterthought.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Whittingham not ready to hand keys over to Wilson yet
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - With so much uncertainty as to when Cam Rising will be able to return at quarterback for the Utah football team, is there a point when head coach Kyle Whittingham will move on from Rising and hand the keys to the offense over to freshman Isaac Wilson? "There is
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah HC players understand significance of first NHL game
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - No matter what happens this season, there will never be another first NHL game for the Utah Hockey Club It's just game 1 of 82, but the Utah HC players know how historic tonight's game against the Chicago Blackhawks at the Delta Center will be.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Clayton Kershaw won't pitch for the Dodgers in playoffs after big toe injury fails to heal
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Clayton Kershaw, sidelined since late August with a toe injury, won’t pitch again this season for the Los Angeles Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers' Kershaw, Guardians' Lively, Tigers' Mize and Yankees' Stroman are left off ALDS rosters
item.description: Pitchers Clayton Kershaw and Joe Kelly were left off the Los Angeles Dodgers' roster for their NL Division Series against the San Diego Padres that started Saturday. San Diego added right-handers Martín Pérez and Alek Jacob, who were skipped from the Wild Card Series roster against Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utes to play baseball at Smith's Ballpark next season
item.description: The University of Utah has partnered up with Salt Lake City and the Salt Lake Bees to have the Utes play baseball at Smith's Ballpark during the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Is the Chicago Bears run game back?
item.description: The Chicago Bears are relying on their running game and short passing game to replicate their Week 4 performance against the Carolina Panthers, as they have shifted away from a run-dominant offense under their new offensive coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Patrick Fishburn ready to end season on high note
item.description: When the Black Desert Championship tees off next week in St George, former Fremont High School, and BYU star Patrick Fishburn will be among the golfers playing in the event.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Ruff, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark a near-unanimous choice as WNBA's Rookie of the Year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP/WGN) — Caitlin Clark has been named the WNBA Rookie of the Year in a near-unanimous vote, giving the Indiana Fever back-to-back winners after Aliyah Boston won the honor last season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
